channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: TCU, Duke climb into top 10, Notre Dame drops in women's AP Top 25; UCLA and UConn remain 1-2
item.description: TCU has grabbed its best ranking ever in The Associated Press Top 25 women's basketball poll on Monday after a convincing win over Notre Dame as the rankings got a shakeup following a holiday tournament week marked by key losses. The Horned Frogs climbed eight spots to No.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Golden State Valkyries unveil season games, home opener
item.description: (KRON) – The newest Bay Area major pro sports team, the Golden State Valkyries, has announced the date of its inaugural season at the Chase Center on Monday. The inaugural game, slated for May 16 of next year, will be a California battle with the Valkyries facing the Los Angeles Sparks.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Baker, KRON4

item.title: Hall of Famer Randy Moss reveals he's 'battling something' internal and asks for prayers
item.description: Hall of Fame wide receiver Randy Moss revealed he's dealing with a health issue and asked fans to pray for him and his family. The 47-year-old ESPN football analyst made the announcement on Instagram from the set of the network’s “Sunday NFL Countdown” show.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 49ers' Christian McCaffrey suffered 'potentially' season-ending knee injury vs Bills, coach says
item.description: San Francisco 49ers star Christian McCaffrey could "potentially" be lost for the season with a knee injury, head coach Kyle Shanahan said Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ESPN's Kirk Herbstreit demands stiffer penalties for players involved in flag fights
item.description: ESPN college football analyst Kirk Herbstreit called for steeper consequences for those involved in fights over flag-planting incidents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Houston's Al-Shaair apologizes for hit on Jacksonville's Lawrence that led to concussion
item.description: Houston’s Azeez Al-Shaair took to X to apologize to Jacksonville’s Trevor Lawrence after his violent blow to the quarterback’s facemask led to him being carted off the field with a concussion.
item.dc:creator: Kristie Rieken, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jazz come up short against Lakers, 105-104
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Anthony Davis had 33 points and 11 rebounds, LeBron James added 27 points and 14 assists, and the short-handed Los Angeles Lakers beat the Utah Jazz 105-104 on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Mitchell scores 35, Cavs end Celtics' winning streak with 115-111 win in matchup of East's elite
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Donovan Mitchell scored 20 of his 35 points in the fourth quarter as the Cleveland Cavaliers ended Boston's seven-game winning streak with a 115-111 victory over the Celtics on Sunday night in the second intense matchup between the Eastern Conference’s top teams this season.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Big Ten fines Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for postgame melee
item.description: The Big Ten Conference announced it fined Michigan and Ohio State $100,000 each for violating the conference's sportsmanship policy for the on-field melee at the end of the Wolverines' win in Columbus on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Atkins reaches 2,000 yards as USF Bulls fall at Rice
item.description: South Florida (6-6; 4-4 American) fell to Rice (4-8; 3-5 American), 35-28, Saturday as the Bulls closed out the regular season in the program’s first-ever game at Rice Stadium in Houston.
item.dc:creator: USF Football, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Aggies blow 17-point fourth quarter lead in loss to CSU
item.description: FORT COLLINS, Colo. (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah State football team could not hold a 30-13 fourth quarter lead in its season finale at Colorado State, and the Aggies end the season with a 42-37 loss to the Rams.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Bears fire coach Matt Eberflus after skid marked by poor decisions late in games
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Matt Eberflus was fired by the Chicago Bears on Friday, one day after botching a timeout in a loss at Detroit. Offensive coordinator Thomas Brown will serve as interim coach, the Bears said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN and ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Taylor Swift spends Black Friday amid sea of red as boyfriend Travis Kelce's Chiefs face the Raiders
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Pop superstar Taylor Swift was spending Black Friday amid a sea of red to watch her boyfriend, Travis Kelce, and the rest of the Kansas City Chiefs as they played the Las Vegas Raiders in a chilly matinee at Arrowhead Stadium.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 49ers QB Brock Purdy remains severely limited by injury to his throwing shoulder
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — Brock Purdy threw one short pass in the open portion of practice for the San Francisco 49ers as he remains slowed by an injury to his throwing shoulder that has already forced him to miss a game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Depleted Jazz lose to Denver, 122-103
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Without injured stars Lauri Markkanen, John Collins and Jordan Clarkson, the Utah Jazz had little chance against the Denver Nuggets and lost for the sixth time in their last seven games.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: New York Jets hire 33rd team to assist in hiring process
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- An organizational shift is upon us for the New York Jets. Owner Woody Johnson has hired the 33rd team which was founded by former Jets GM Mike Tannenbaum in 2019, to help aid the Jets search.
item.dc:creator: Marc Malusis, PIX 11

item.title: Wembanyama scores 34 as Spurs beat Jazz, 128-115
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - 17 games into the NBA season, the Utah Jazz and San Antonio Spurs have already played each other four times, and the Jazz have to be glad they don't have to see Victor Wembanyama again this season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
